content.text: TypeScript check passes with no errors. Let me also verify the layout file looks correct now:

All files are in order. TypeScript check passes cleanly. Here's a summary of everything done:

## Summary

All 9 steps completed. TypeScript check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) passes with zero errors.

### Files created

- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/admin/leads.ts` — GET endpoint returning quiz leads + stats from the `leads` table, requires auth cookie
- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/admin/health.ts` — GET endpoint checking all services/env vars (DB, Resend, JWT, Admin password, Calendly), requires auth cookie
- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/admin/config.ts` — GET/POST endpoint for site banner config stored in `site_config` table, requires auth cookie
- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/banner.ts` — Public GET endpoint returning banner state (no auth required, used by frontend)

### Files modified

- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/subscribe.ts` — After a successful email send, now inserts the lead into the `leads` table (non-blocking, uses dynamic import to avoid module sharing issues)
- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/admin.tsx` — Full rewrite: 4-tab dashboard (Inicio/Leads/Salud/Config) with stat cards, leads table with category badges, health check list with colored dots, banner config form with toggle/text/type selector. Login form preserved. Maintenance toggle moved to header bar. File is 480 lines.
- `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/components/layout.tsx` — Added `SiteBanner` component that fetches `/api/banner` on mount and renders a dismissible colored bar (blue/amber/green) above all page content when enabled. Removed the hardcoded maintenance alert that was always visible.

### Key design decisions
- Each API file is self-contained (auth helper copied, not imported) per Vercel serverless constraints
- `leads` table is created on first request via `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`
- `site_config` table is shared with the existing maintenance endpoint
- Banner dismissal is local state only (not persisted), reappears on page refresh
